content: Suggest more realistic target months for goals that are currently at risk.

**Re‑targeting the “At Risk” goals** | Goal | Current target | New realistic target | Why | |------|----------------|----------------------|-----| | **Emergency Fund** (High) | 2026‑12 | **2027‑07** | With a projected monthly contribution of **$9,899.50**, the remaining $120,897.10 will take ~12.2 months. 12 months from July 2026 lands in July 2027. | | **Trip** (Medium) | 2026‑10 | **2027‑01** | $60,000 ÷ $9,899.50 ≈ 6.1 months → early January 2027. | | **Gadget** (Low) | 2027‑02 | **2027‑03** | $80,000 ÷ $9,899.50 ≈ 8.1 months → early March 2027. | | **Loan Closure Target** (High) | 2027‑06 | **2028‑03** | $200,000 ÷ $9,899.50 ≈ 20.2 months → March 2028. | --- ### Trade‑offs & next steps | Priority | Trade‑off | Recommendation | |----------|-----------|----------------| | **High (Emergency Fund, Loan Closure)** | Delaying the target reduces the urgency of these critical goals but keeps the savings plan realistic. | • Keep the new target dates.
• If you want to finish earlier, consider increasing the monthly contribution (e.g., by cutting discretionary spending or boosting income). | | **Medium (Trip)** | A later target means you’ll have to start saving earlier, but you’ll avoid cutting into the emergency fund or loan payoff. | • Maintain the new target.
• Allocate the “suggested monthly savings” ($8,571.43) to the trip while still contributing the full $9,899.50 to overall savings. | | **Low (Gadget)** | The gadget goal is least critical; a modest delay is acceptable. | • Keep the new target.
• Use any surplus from the trip or emergency fund to accelerate gadget savings if desired. | --- ### Practical next steps 1. **Update the dashboard** with the new target months (2027‑07, 2027‑01, 2027‑03, 2028‑03). 2. **Re‑allocate the “suggested monthly savings”** amounts to each goal, ensuring the total monthly contribution stays at $9,899.50. 3. **Monitor monthly progress**: every month, compare actual savings to the projected contribution to catch any drift early. 4. **If you can increase your monthly contribution** (e.g., by $1,000–$2,000), recalculate the target dates—this will bring the high‑priority goals back on track sooner. By aligning the target months with the realistic pace of your current savings, you keep all goals attainable while protecting the most critical ones.
